content.verdict: Kirini (part of The Leading Hotels of the World / LUX Collection) is Oia luxury done with restraint — 23 suites arranged on the caldera with a stunning infinity pool, a serious spa, and one of the village's best fine-dining restaurants. The design is whiter-than-white minimalism with jewel-toned accents, and the service is among the most polished on the island. Kirini is strictly adults-only, which gives it a hushed, grown-up atmosphere rare in Oia's peak-season bustle. For honeymooners who want the Oia sunset address with five-star service guarantees (and don't want to spend Perivolas money), Kirini is the obvious pick.
content.best_room: The Kirini Suite with heated outdoor plunge pool — a spacious cave suite with private terrace, plunge pool facing the caldera, and a view calibrated for sunset. For a signature splurge, the Grand Kirini Suite adds a separate living area and a larger private pool.

itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning hike from Oia toward Finikia, beach afternoon at Ammoudi Bay with seafood lunch at Dimitris Ammoudi Taverna, dinner back at the hotel.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Slow breakfast, late checkout, private airport transfer
itinerary_7_nights.description: Slow breakfast, late checkout, private airport transfer.

caveats: Oia gets very crowded from late afternoon — stay on property 5-8pm in peak season.
The hotel is on the quieter edge of Oia but still involves steps throughout.
Peak-season pricing is steep; shoulder-season value (May/October) is excellent.
Some lower-tier suites have partial view — always confirm specifically.
The spa is good but not enormous; book treatments when you reserve the room.

faqs.a: Yes, 16+. Part of why honeymooners love it.

faqs.q: How does Kirini compare to Andronis Luxury Suites?
faqs.a: Kirini is slightly smaller-feeling, with a more polished spa; Andronis has a more famous restaurant (Lycabettus). Both are top-tier Oia choices.
Most are partially cave-style with vaulted ceilings. A few newer units are more contemporary. Ask for your preference.

faqs.q: How far is Kirini from Oia's main square?
faqs.a: A 5-10 minute walk along the caldera path, depending on your suite's location within the property.

faqs.q: What's the best time to visit?
faqs.a: Late May-June and September-early October for the best balance of weather, light, and manageable crowds.

faqs.q: Is the on-site restaurant worth it?
faqs.a: Yes — one of the best in Oia. Reserve for one or two of your dinners, especially sunset.
